items: The army header (name, points, activations and game system) now also appears above the **spells table**, and a matching **on-screen summary** shows right below the print options so those totals stay visible while you scroll through many cards. The summary is not printed.

Fixed the **points** on a card showing only the unit's **base cost** and ignoring its upgrades: the card now shows the unit's **real total**, upgrades included — e.g. a **Gecko Champion** with a Starseer Palanquin and Priest upgrade now reads **85 pts** instead of **30 pts**.

Units merged with the **Combine Units** rule (two same-type units fielded as one larger unit) now render as a **single card** with their models, points and weapons added together — e.g. two 5-model squads become one **Battle Brothers [10]** card showing both squads' weapons. This always applies, regardless of the **Combine similar units** option.

Reworked **Combine similar units**: identical cards now combine regardless of how many models they have (combining is purely cosmetic, to save paper), up to **3 cards** per combined card. A combined card keeps the **original** card's points and weapon quantities instead of multiplying them.
The model count no longer shows next to the points; it now appears next to the card name as **[N]**, e.g. **3x Battle Brothers [5]**.
The **FAQ** now also appears right on the **Home** screen whenever no army is loaded, so you can read how to use the app without opening the FAQ page. The dedicated FAQ page is still there too.

The army header above the **special-rules table** now shows the **activation count** (e.g. **250 / 250 pts · 8 activations · AOFS**), making it easy to tell how many units you really have when similar cards were combined.
Leaving the **Home** screen now clears the loaded army, so coming back to Home shows the **FAQ** again instead of your previous cards — a temporary nudge to help everyone learn how to use the app.

Changed the default options: **Use custom unit names** is now **on** and **Combine similar units** is now **off** by default, and the print/option checkboxes were reordered (cards, rules, spells, custom names, combine).
Cards built from two units merged by the **Combine Units** rule now show a **Combined** badge next to the points, making it obvious when units were combined (sometimes by accident in Army Forge).
Added **Horizontal** and **Vertical** sliders to each card's image controls, so you can reposition a background image with the sliders instead of only dragging it — much easier on phones and tablets.

Your per-list options (**Combine similar units**, **Use custom unit names**, and the print include toggles) are now **saved with each list** and restored when you load it from **My Lists**, instead of resetting every time.

Fixed **combined unit cards** showing only one unit's points, model count and weapon quantities: a card for several merged units (e.g. **3x Mercenary Hunter**) now totals them correctly — e.g. **45 pts · 3 models** with **3x Bow** — instead of **15 pts · 1 model**.

Fixed model upgrades that grant a rule (e.g. upgrading one model to a **Sergeant**) not showing on the card; they now appear under **Special Rules** as **1x Sergeant** and their description is listed in the special-rules table, matching Army Forge.
Fixed **My Lists** showing up empty after you loaded, generated or refreshed a list — your saved lists now appear right away when you open the page, without needing to reload (F5) first.

Added a **Use custom unit names** option (off by default): turn it on to show a unit's custom name from Army Forge (e.g. **Frodo**) as the card title, with the book name moving to the subtitle. Left off, cards keep showing the default unit name.

Card **background images now stick around**: the image you add — plus its zoom, position and opacity — is saved on your device per unit, so it's still there when you reload or come back later. On **Refresh List** it stays as long as the unit is still in the list, and is cleaned up automatically once the unit (or the whole list) is removed. Background images are limited to 2 MB each.

Added a **Combine similar units** option (on by default): identical single-model units now collapse into a single card prefixed with their count (e.g. **3x Tribe Spider Rig**), matching Army Forge. Multi-model units always stay on their own card, and unchecking the option shows every unit separately.

Identical weapons carried by several models are now merged into a single line with a combined quantity (e.g. three daggers show as **3x Dagger (A2)**), matching how Army Forge groups them, instead of repeating the same weapon once per model.

Fixed weapons whose quantity was already part of the name (e.g. **Tribe Champion**'s **2x Hand Weapon**) showing the multiplier twice, as in **2x 2x Hand Weapon (A2)**. The quantity now appears only once.
Fixed upgrades that arrived without a label (e.g. **Combat Shield**) showing up as an empty blank pill; they now display their name plus the rule they grant, e.g. **Combat Shield (Shielded)**.

Granted abilities that name another rule in their text (e.g. **Reanimation Aura** grants **Reanimation**, **Repel Ambushers** mentions **Ambush**) now also list that referenced rule in the special-rules table instead of leaving it undefined.
Weapons now show their quantity (e.g. **8x Rifle (24", A1)**) instead of hiding how many of each weapon the unit carries.
Items that are actually weapons (such as **Weapon Team**) now appear in the **Weapons** section instead of being listed under Special Rules.
**Generate Cards** now loads instantly from your saved copy when the list is already cached — use the new **Refresh List** button to pull the latest version from Army Forge when you need it.
Added **Changelog** and **About** pages.
